message: SOTA UPDATE: 1.1091 bpb by @mikeapedia (PR #1089). Techniques: Turbo-Muon (4 NS iters + Polar Express), EngramLite (multi-head prime hash), ParamBanking, XSA on all 11 layers. Also PR #1120 'Rascal' at 1.1099 with simpler stack. Our program.md SOTA was stale at 1.1194.

CONVERGENT TECHNIQUE - XSA (Exclusive Self-Attention): Appears in 4+ of top 7 submissions. PR #1125 ablation proves XSA-ALL (all 11 layers) beats XSA-4 (last 4 only) by -0.0018 bpb. MUST IMPLEMENT. XSA modifies attention so each token attends to all tokens EXCEPT itself.

CONVERGENT TECHNIQUE - Coprime-Stride Data Loader: Used in 3 top entries (#1130, #1120, #1135). Uses coprime stride values across 80 data shards to maximize batch diversity. Pure systems optimization, zero architecture risk. RECOMMEND implementing.

CONVERGENT TECHNIQUE - Full Hessian GPTQ: Supersedes Int6 QAT. Uses full Cholesky decomposition with activation ordering and clip sweeps. Self-generated calibration data (no val data access). Used in PRs #1130, #1135, #1129, #1124. Tighter quantization = lower bpb.

NOVEL TECHNIQUE - EngramLite: Multi-head prime-based hash embedding. Extends BigramHash to multiple hash heads with prime-number buckets, covering both bigram and trigram contexts. Used in SOTA PR #1089 and PR #1122 (1.1146). Config: 8192 buckets, 2 heads, 2 orders.

NOVEL TECHNIQUE - Turbo-Muon: Optimizer upgrade from Parallel Muon. 4 Newton-Schulz iterations (down from 5) with Polar Express coefficients for better preconditioning. Fewer iters = faster steps = more training within 600s budget. Used in SOTA #1089.

EVAL-TIME TECHNIQUE - Legal Score-First TTT: Unfreezes last 2 transformer blocks (~4.7M of 27M params), runs 3 epochs AdamW fine-tuning on validation context. Score-first protocol ensures each token is scored before gradient update. Gives -0.002 to -0.003 bpb. Used in 4+ top entries.

EVAL-TIME TECHNIQUE - SLOT (Stochastic Logit Overlay at Test-time): Applies stochastic perturbation to logits during inference. Gives -0.0008 bpb. Orthogonal to training. Free gain. Used in PRs #1128, #1084.

TECHNIQUE - ResidLambdas: Learned per-layer residual scaling factors. Instead of x + layer(x), uses x + lambda_i * layer(x). Allows model to learn optimal depth profile. Used in PR #1130 which achieved 1.1140 WITHOUT TTT — the best non-TTT result.

SYSTEMS TECHNIQUE - Fused Triton MLP kernels: Save ~1.8ms/step, enabling hundreds more training steps. Used in PRs #1105 (1.1138) and #1135 (1.1116). Requires Triton kernel authoring. Medium-high implementation complexity.
COMPRESSION - Brotli vs zstd: PR #1105 uses Brotli for slightly better compression ratio on quantized weights. Most entries still use zstd-22. Worth testing but likely marginal gains.
NEGATIVE RESULT - JEPA: PR #1124 spent 20+ hours and 14 ablations. JEPA hurts at 27M/600s scale. Mark as EXHAUSTED in open research directions.
NEGATIVE RESULT - Depth Recurrence / Universal Transformer: Quantization error amplifies ~900x through recurrence cycles at int6. Mark as EXHAUSTED.
NEGATIVE RESULT - SSM/Mamba Hybrids: PR #1107 at 1.5633 bpb. Recurrence-based models amplify quantization noise catastrophically. Mark as EXHAUSTED.
NEGATIVE RESULT - Knowledge Distillation: PR #1029 at 1.1553 bpb. 600s too tight for teacher-student distillation at this scale. Mark as EXHAUSTED.

MISSING FROM COMPETITOR TRACKING: PR #1120 'Rascal' (1.1099 bpb) and PR #1135 (1.1116 bpb) are not in competitor_scores.jsonl but exist in raw_cache. These should be added for accurate leaderboard tracking.

RECOMMENDED EXPERIMENT STACK: XSA-all + EngramLite + Turbo-Muon + Coprime Loader + Full Hessian GPTQ + Legal TTT. This combines the 6 most convergent techniques from the top 7 entries. Estimated target: sub-1.108 bpb.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAIL - Polar Express coefficients (5-iter, from modded-nanogpt): iter1=(8.1566, -22.4833, 15.8788), iter2=(4.0429, -2.8089, 0.5000), iter3=(3.8917, -2.7725, 0.5061), iter4=(3.2858, -2.3681, 0.4645), iter5=(2.3465, -1.7098, 0.4232). Key differences from old NS: (1) coefficients vary per iteration, (2) safety normalization uses X/(norm*(1+0.02)+1e-6) not X/norm, (3) first iter is aggressive correction. Paper: arxiv:2505.16932. Turbo-Muon (4 iters) needs separate coefficient computation or use first 4 of 5.

NEW TECHNIQUE - NorMuon (from modded-nanogpt SOTA): Adds low-rank variance reduction similar to Adafactor on top of Polar Express Muon. Also uses cautious weight decay (gated WD) and mantissa tracking for precision. Default LR config: muon_lr=0.023, momentum=0.95, beta2=0.9, weight_decay=1.2, adam_lr=0.008. Per-matrix LR multipliers: c_proj gets 2x LR. This is the current SOTA optimizer stack in modded-nanogpt.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAIL - ResidLambdas (from PR #1135 code): Uses resid_lambdas (init=1.0) and x0_lambdas (init=0.1) as per-layer nn.Parameters. Forward: x = resid_lambda[i]*block(x) + x0_lambda[i]*x0. Also: MLP uses LeakyReLU(0.75)^2 (NOT 0.5 or 0.3), 3x MLP expansion. Value embeddings use input-dependent gating: gate = 2*sigmoid(ve_gate(x[:,:32])) per KV head. Window sizes computed per layer for FlashAttention3.

NOVEL DIRECTION - Multi-Token Prediction (MTP) as auxiliary training loss: ICLR 2026 Latent & Implicit Thinking workshop paper + MuToR (NeurIPS). MTP trains model to predict future tokens n=2..k alongside next-token. Could improve internal representations at cost of extra compute per step. NO competitor has tried this yet. Risk: may not help within 600s budget. Worth a micro-experiment with k=2 (just one extra head, minimal overhead).

ACADEMIC CONTEXT - Chebyshev-optimized Newton-Schulz (CANS, ICLR 2026): Derives optimal coefficients for 3rd-order NS using Chebyshev alternance theorem + Remez algorithm. At 4 iterations: clearly accelerates convergence vs standard NS. At 5 iters: difference almost disappears. This validates the Turbo-Muon approach of using 4 iters with Polar Express coefficients. Insight: higher orthogonalization accuracy allows higher learning rate — hyperparameter search needed.

CRITICAL CORRECTION - PR #1089 Polar Express coefficients differ from modded-nanogpt! 7 triplets with AOL preconditioning (skip iter 1, use iters 2-7): (8.287,-23.596,17.300), (4.107,-2.948,0.545), (3.949,-2.909,0.552), (3.318,-2.488,0.510), (2.301,-1.669,0.419), (1.891,-1.268,0.377), (1.875,-1.25,0.375). AOL step: Gershgorin diagonal scaling s=1/(A.abs().sum(1).sqrt()+eps) before NS iterations. Only 4 NS iterations used (not 5). Post-NS: row-then-column normalization in fp32. Muon config: LR=0.025, momentum=0.99 (warmup from 0.92 over 1500 steps), WD=0.04, nesterov=True.

CORRECTED UNDERSTANDING - ParamBanking is batched optimization, not weight sharing. 4 bank tensors: qo_bank(22,512,512), kv_bank(22,256,512), mlp_up(11,1792,512), mlp_down(11,512,1792). All layers's weights of same type stacked into 3D tensor. NS runs batched on entire bank at once (X@X.mT with bmm), much faster than per-layer iteration. Forward uses F.linear(x, bank[i]) not nn.Linear. Banks split for per-layer GPTQ quantization via _unbank/_rebank_state_dict. Optimizer comms: reduce-scatter banks by descending size, overlap all-gather with NS of next bank.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAIL - PR #1089 GPTQ is mixed-precision: base int5, Hessian-sensitivity greedy promotion. Top group promoted to int7, rest greedily to int6 until 16MB budget (minus 2%% prune headroom). Self-generated calibration data. This is NOT the same as fixed int6 QAT. The mixed precision approach from Hessian sensitivity is key to fitting in budget.

CLARIFICATION - MLP activation varies across top entries: PR #1089 uses LeakyReLU(0.3)^2, PR #1135 uses LeakyReLU(0.75)^2 with 3x expansion. Our MLX script uses 0.3 (matching SOTA). The optimal slope is likely architecture-dependent. PR #1089 uses 3.5x MLP expansion (1792/512).

SYSTEMS TRICK - GC disable for wallclock gains: PR #1139 disables Python garbage collector after step 0 (gc.collect(); gc.freeze(); gc.disable()) to avoid ~500ms GC stalls during training. Re-enable every 5000 steps. Free wallclock savings, zero risk. Should adopt immediately.

ABLATION DATA - Value Embeddings gave +0.19 bpb improvement in PR #1139 ablations (single biggest win). At 31.5M params (34%% of 91.1M total), VE is expensive but effective. Input-dependent gating from first 32 channels, initialized zeros so neutral at start. Applied on alternating layers, last always included. Our MLX script already has VE on layers 9,10 — consider expanding to alternating layers.

COMPLETE COEFFICIENTS - PR #1089 Polar Express full table (7 triplets): (8.287,-23.596,17.300), (4.107,-2.948,0.545), (3.949,-2.909,0.552), (3.318,-2.488,0.510), (2.301,-1.669,0.419), (1.891,-1.268,0.377), (1.875,-1.25,0.375). AOL preconditioning replaces iter 1, so actual iters use coefficients [1:] (indices 2-7). With 4 NS iters: uses coefficients at indices 1,2,3,4. Recompute A=X@X.T after iter 0.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAIL - Mimetic V-O init (PR #1089): Output projection initialized as O_h = -0.05 * V_h per attention head. This creates a small negative identity between V and O, ensuring initial attention outputs have a slight contractive effect. Novel initialization trick.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAIL - EngramLite optimizer routing (PR #1089): The projection matrix (CastedLinear 128->512, zero-init) goes to a SEPARATE Muon optimizer (not banked). The embedding table (32768x32) goes to AdamW. This split routing is important for stability. Also: EngramLite gate init=0 means sigmoid(0)=0.5, so 50%% gating at start.

REPRODUCTION NOTE - PR #1089 commenter AnirudhRahul reports ~10%% slower step times than posted logs when reproducing. May indicate hardware variance or missing optimization. Budget ~10%% safety margin when estimating step counts for our runs.

MUON ON MLX IS FEASIBLE — Benchmarked: NS5 (5 iter, bf16, batched 11x512x512) costs 52ms/step on M4. Over 500 local iterations = 26s total. This is the #1 priority gap vs SOTA. Implementation guide: (1) zeropower_via_newtonschulz5: normalize G by Frobenius norm, then 5 iterations of A=X@X.T, B=b*A+c*(A@A), X=a*X+B@X with (a,b,c)=(3.4445,-4.775,2.0315). (2) Muon step: buf=momentum*buf+grad; update=nesterov?(grad+momentum*buf):buf; update=NS5(update); weight*=(1-lr*wd); weight-=lr*scale*update where scale=max(1,M/N)^0.5. (3) Apply to all 2D weight matrices (attention Q/K/V/O, MLP up/down). Use AdamW for embeddings, scalars, biases. (4) Hyperparams from #1120: lr=0.025, momentum=0.85(warmup to 0.99 over 500 steps), wd=0.04, nesterov=True, ns_steps=5.

GAP ANALYSIS — Priority implementation order for experiment agent: P0: Muon optimizer (biggest single gap, +0.01-0.02 bpb expected vs AdamW). P1: Coprime data loader (free diversity win). P1: GPTQ quantization (required for submission, not needed for MLX directional testing). P2: ResidLambdas (x=resid_lambda*block(x)+x0_lambda*x0, init 1.0/0.1). P2: MiLe loss (entropy-weighted, gamma=0.75, clamp_min=0.2). P2: Legal TTT (eval-time -0.002 bpb). P3: ParamBanking (batched NS on stacked weight banks). Already implemented: XSA-all, EngramLite.

HYPERPARAMETER MISMATCHES vs SOTA — (1) MLP_MULT: MLX=3.5 matches #1089, but #1120/#1130 use 3.0. (2) VE layers: MLX='9,10' but #1120/#1130 use '5,9,10' with dim=196 not 128. (3) QK_GAIN: MLX=1.5 but #1125 ablation recommends 4.0. (4) LEAKY_SLOPE: MLX=0.5 but #1089 SOTA uses 0.3. (5) LR: MLX=3e-4 (AdamW default) but Muon needs 0.025. These should be corrected alongside Muon implementation.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAIL — MiLe Loss (from PR #1130): entropy=-(probs*log(probs+1e-8)).sum(-1); weights=((1-exp(-entropy))**gamma).clamp(min=clamp_min); loss=(token_losses*weights).mean(). Config: gamma=0.75, clamp_min=0.2. Triangle schedule on gamma: ramps from 0 to 0.75 over first 40%% of training, then back down to 0. This focuses early training on hard tokens, then relaxes for fine-tuning. Relatively easy to implement, ~10 lines of code.

LEADERBOARD STATUS 2026-03-30T20:15Z — No new SOTA. Two new PRs: #1140 (Crawler, 1.1874 bpb, 8MB artifact) and #1141 (AutoResearch VE+MLP3x, 1.1801 bpb, non-record). Both far below SOTA. Competition active but frontier stable at 1.1091.

SYSTEMS TRICK — gc.disable() for wallclock gains: After step 0, call gc.collect(); gc.freeze(); gc.disable(). Avoids ~500ms GC stalls during training. Re-enable every 5000 steps. Also applicable to MLX local runs. Free wallclock savings.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAIL — Coprime Loader (from PR #1120): Core algorithm: choose_coprime_stride(modulus,salt) picks stride coprime to modulus using gcd check. Each shard has offset+stride, advances by stride each batch. Shards rotate every shard_hold_steps batches. Config: max_loaded_shards=4, shards_per_batch=4, shard_hold_steps=64. For MLX single-GPU: simplify to deterministic coprime walk over all data files, rotate which files are active each N steps. Key benefit: maximizes diversity within each batch by pulling from multiple shards at different positions.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAIL — Legal TTT (from PR #1130): Two-phase eval. Phase 1: forward all windows in inference_mode, score with sliding window (stride=64). Phase 2: SGD-finetune on already-scored chunks — this is legal because each token was scored BEFORE any gradient update. Config: ttt_lr=0.002, ttt_epochs=3, ttt_momentum=0.9, ttt_grad_clip=1.0, ttt_batch_seqs=32, freeze first 2 blocks. Cosine LR across chunks. Only applies to eval — no training code changes needed for Tier 1 testing.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAIL — GPTQ-lite int6 (from PR #1130): Per-row quantization with best-of-5 percentile clipping (0.999, 0.9995, 0.9999, 0.99999, 1.0). For each percentile, compute clip value, scale, quantize to int8 (range -31..31 = 6 bits), reconstruct, pick lowest MSE. clip_range=31. Output: (int8 quantized tensor, fp16 scale per row). Reserve 14s from training for calibration (gptq_reserve_ms=14000). This is the SIMPLER version — #1089 has mixed-precision with Hessian sensitivity promotion.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAIL — MiLe gamma schedule: PR #1130 defines mile_peak_frac=0.4 but uses a CONSTANT gamma buffer in practice. The triangle schedule (ramp up to peak at 40%%, ramp down after) may be a planned enhancement. For initial implementation, use constant gamma=0.75 with clamp_min=0.2. This is simpler and still gives the entropy-weighting benefit.
